opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is tied in knots, I can't leave the toilet, and I'm so exhausted I feel faint.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A really bad flare-up of my gut started last night — cramping, watery diarrhoea I can't control, and now I feel like I might pass out when I stand up.

symptom_narrative.onset: started yesterday evening with a lot of bloating, then got much worse over about 12 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: since last night — roughly 18 hours and worse than when it began
symptom_narrative.character: gripping, cramping pain that grabs the whole belly, with sudden urgent watery diarrhoea
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the cramps are agonising and come in waves; I feel wiped out and shaky
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over my belly, not one spot
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in waves, worse each time I try to eat or drink
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much has helped; lying still eases it a little between waves
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: even a sip of water sets off painful cramps and sends me back to the toilet
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling faint and dizzy when I stand, really drained, dry mouth, and I felt sick and threw up when I tried a tablet for the diarrhoea
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had ongoing gut trouble since a bad bout of food poisoning about 6 months ago — my GP said my bowel is oversensitive now. I get flares like this that settle down after a few days, and this feels like the same thing but stronger
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: stuck in the bathroom, can't stand up properly without getting dizzy

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: sensitive/oversensitive bowel since food poisoning 6 months ago, with repeated flares that settle after a few days

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: none regular; tried an over-the-counter anti-diarrhoea tablet today but vomited it straight back up

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; worried about falling because of the dizziness while going back and forth to the toilet

objective_data_offered.value: my smartwatch says my heart rate is around 105-110 and it feels like it's racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single wearable reading while distressed and moving between toilet and bed — may be affected by anxiety and motion

objective_data_offered.value: on video the patient looks pale and clammy, is clutching the abdomen, and has visibly dry, cracked lips
patient reports still passing watery stool and wind, and is drinking (or trying to) from unopened water bottles kept nearby
